# WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0002 - RFC 9000 Address-Validation Token Emission Work Item

## Summary

Keep the RFC 9000 path-validation slice honest while the runtime gains server-only NEW_TOKEN emission and per-address deduplication.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0005
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0011

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0002

## Planned Changes

- Add a server-only token-emission record keyed by remote address.
- Flush NEW_TOKEN frames after new-address validation once a protected 1-RTT send is possible.
- Keep repeated validation of the same address from creating duplicate NEW_TOKEN traffic.
- Keep the framing helper internal to the runtime and avoid public API widening.

## Out of Scope

- Retry-token handling or any broader token-management API.
- Public API widening for token issuance or token validation.
- Client-role token emission.

## Verification Plan

Run the RFC 9000 requirement-home tests for the two token-emission clauses and confirm the new-token payload helper round-trips through the frame codec.

## Completion Notes

Optional implementation notes, deviations, or follow-up items.
